explanation: The natural language statement specifies a condition that must always hold when the system is in PCV Mode. It states that whenever a new breathing cycle begins while in PCV Mode, the inspiration phase must concurrently start. The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures this property holds at all times. The implication `(PCVMode & breathingCycleStart) -> inspiratoryPhaseStart` captures the 'if-then' relationship: if the system is in PCV mode and a breathing cycle starts, then the inspiratory phase must also start at that very moment. This accurately reflects the 'shall start with' phrase, implying immediate or simultaneous initiation.

The natural language statement specifies a condition that must hold when the system is in 'PCV Mode'. The core requirement is that a 'breath cycle' must 'start with the inspiration phase'. This translates to a global property (G). Inside the global scope, we check if the system is in 'PCVMode'. If it is, then whenever a 'breathingCycleStart' occurs, it must be immediately accompanied by 'inspiratoryPhaseStart'. The implication (->) captures this immediate co-occurrence: if the breath cycle starts, then the inspiration phase must also start at that very moment.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'PCVMode', then whenever a 'breathingCycleStart' occurs, it must be accompanied by 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' at the same moment. This directly captures the requirement that a breath cycle in PCV Mode 'shall start with the inspiration phase', implying simultaneity of the cycle's beginning and the inspiration phase's beginning.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode) and a breathing cycle starts (breathingCycleStart), then it must be true that the inspiratory phase also starts (inspiratoryPhaseStart) at the same time. This precisely captures the requirement that in PCV mode, the initiation of a new breath cycle is synchronized with the beginning of the inspiratory phase.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode and a breathing cycle starts (breathingCycleStart), then the inspiratory phase must also start at that exact moment (inspiratoryPhaseStart). This captures the requirement that the initiation of a breath cycle in PCV mode is always marked by the commencement of the inspiration phase.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode), then the start of a breathing cycle (breathingCycleStart) must coincide with the start of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseStart). The biconditional (<->) ensures that whenever a breathing cycle begins, the inspiratory phase also begins at that exact moment, and conversely, whenever an inspiratory phase begins, it signifies the start of a new breathing cycle. This precisely captures the natural language requirement that 'the breath cycle shall start with the inspiration phase' by enforcing their simultaneous occurrence when in PCV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV Mode, then whenever a breathing cycle begins, the inspiratory phase must also begin at that exact moment. This directly captures the requirement that the breath cycle initiates with the inspiration phase when the system is operating in PCV Mode.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode, then whenever a breathing cycle starts (breathingCycleStart), the inspiratory phase must also start at the very same time (inspiratoryPhaseStart). This directly captures the requirement that the breath cycle's beginning in PCV mode is synonymous with the start of the inspiratory phase.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV mode, then whenever a breathing cycle starts, the inspiratory phase must also start at that very moment. This captures the requirement that in PCV Mode, the breath cycle initiates with the inspiration phase, meaning the start of the breathing cycle is coincident with the start of the inspiratory phase.

The natural language statement specifies a condition that must hold when the system is in 'PCV Mode'. Therefore, the LTL property is universally quantified (G) and conditioned on 'PCVMode'. The core requirement is that 'The breath cycle shall start with the inspiration phase'. This means that whenever a 'breathingCycleStart' event occurs, an 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' event must also occur at the same time. This is captured by the implication 'breathingCycleStart -> inspiratoryPhaseStart'. Combining these, the LTL asserts that globally, if the system is in PCVMode, then the start of a breathing cycle implies the simultaneous start of the inspiratory phase.
